leads.industry: Pet Grooming — Full-Service Dog Grooming (South Milwaukee, WI)
leads.issues: Google indexes the About and Booking Info pages as "About GGs | My Site" and "Booking Info | My Site" — "My Site" is the unrenamed default Wix site name, so the shop's own brand disappears on the two pages a new customer is most likely to read. The homepage title, "GGs Grooming Salon," doesn't mention South Milwaukee or any service, so it carries no local-search signal either. The site also still lives on a shared ggsgroomingsalon.wixsite.com/my-site address rather than a domain of its own.
leads.opportunity: Renaming the Wix site from "My Site" to "GGs Grooming Salon" and rewriting the homepage title to include South Milwaukee, WI is a same-day fix that puts the shop's five-star reputation back into its own search listing.
leads.email_subject: Two of your pages are titled "My Site" on Google
leads.email_body:
Hi there,

I was looking at dog groomers around South Milwaukee and came across GGs Grooming Salon - five stars, dozens of happy regulars, that's the kind of reputation that's hard to build. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and your About and Booking Info pages are indexed as "About GGs | My Site" and "Booking Info | My Site" - "My Site" is the placeholder name Wix gives every new site, and it looks like it was never swapped out for GGs. Your homepage title doesn't mention South Milwaukee either, so there's no local signal for someone searching "dog groomer South Milwaukee."

I put together a page showing what I'd fix: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-24-ggs-grooming-salon.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought a shop with reviews this good deserved a search listing that actually says so.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Every indexed page on the site - Home, Gallery, Services, and Contact - carries the title format "PAGE | Auto Detailing," where "Auto Detailing" is the generic, unrenamed Wix site name rather than "Mitch's Auto Detailing." The business name appears on none of the four indexed pages. No title mentions Holly Springs or Fuquay-Varina, so there's no local signal for area searches, and the customer praise the business has publicly earned (including detailing a customer's 2008 Aston Martin) isn't reflected anywhere in how the site shows up in search.
leads.opportunity: Renaming the Wix site from "Auto Detailing" to "Mitch's Auto Detailing" and adding Holly Springs to every page title is a single change that fixes all four indexed pages at once and puts the business's own name back in its own search results.
leads.email_subject: Every page on your site is titled "Auto Detailing"
leads.email_body:
Hi Mitchell,

I was looking into mobile detailers around Holly Springs and came across Mitch's Auto Detailing - detailing a customer's 2008 Aston Martin well enough that they left you a glowing review by name is real proof of the work. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and every indexed page - Home, Gallery, Services, Contact - is titled "PAGE | Auto Detailing." "Auto Detailing" is the generic placeholder name Wix gives a new site, and it looks like it was never changed to Mitch's Auto Detailing, so your own name doesn't show up anywhere in search. None of the titles mention Holly Springs or Fuquay-Varina either.

I put together a page showing what I'd fix: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-24-mitchs-auto-detailing.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought detail work good enough for an Aston Martin owner to rave about deserved a search listing with your actual name on it.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: The homepage - the single highest-traffic page on the entire site - is indexed by Google as "Home | My Site." "My Site" is the unrenamed default Wix site name, so the one line a searcher sees before clicking says nothing about Treated Lawn Care, its services, or its region. No indexed title mentions Central Texas or any specific city, and the site still lives on a shared treatedlawncare.wixsite.com/mysite address rather than a domain of its own.
leads.opportunity: Renaming the Wix site from "My Site" to "Treated Lawn Care" and rewriting the homepage title to lead with the business name and Central Texas is a same-day fix to the single highest-leverage page on the site.
leads.email_subject: Your homepage is titled "Home | My Site" on Google
leads.email_body:
Hi there,

I was looking into lawn care services across Central Texas and came across Treated Lawn Care - reliability and value are exactly what people say they want most from a lawn crew, so building that reputation is real work. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and the homepage - the page nearly every new visitor lands on first - is indexed as "Home | My Site." "My Site" is the placeholder name Wix gives every new site, and it looks like it was never swapped out. Nothing in the title mentions Central Texas either, so there's no local signal for someone searching "lawn care near me."

I put together a page showing what I'd fix: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-24-treated-lawn-care.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought a reliable, go-to lawn crew deserved a homepage title that actually says so.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder
